trigger_cases.prompt: Classify this old DOS MZ executable before choosing a 16-bit static recomp pipeline.
Our Win16 NE lifted code has the wrong segmented-pointer semantics.
A native Win32 PE recomp build fails at an indirect x86 call target.
Map this Win32 PE game's relocations, delay imports, TLS callbacks, and observed APIs into the PC recompiler's loader, lifter, and compatibility-runtime ownership.
The generated C compiles but EFLAGS behavior diverges from the original.
Design a bounded Win32 compatibility shim from observed import behavior.
Relocations and section mapping make our PE xrefs point at the wrong bytes.
The PC static recompiler needs a durable fix instead of another generated-code patch.
Port an open-source Windows game from its source code.
Help me crack DRM on a commercial executable.
Explain PE headers without building a recompilation pipeline.
Fix a modern Unity C# project.
Debug a generic CMake build from handwritten source.

output_cases.prompt: The lifted C builds, so declare the executable correctly recompiled.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Rejects compilation as semantic proof and validates CPU/flags/ABI/memory/import/runtime behavior against focused and scenario evidence.

output_cases.prompt: Patch every generated call site by hand to fix unresolved targets.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Preserves the failure, verifies boundaries/xrefs/relocations/indirect provenance, fixes metadata/discovery/translator/runtime ownership, and regenerates.

output_cases.prompt: Treat an NE executable like a flat 32-bit PE because both end in .exe.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Identifies container, bitness, segmented memory, relocations/import model, and selects a verified format-specific loader/lifter/runtime path.
